children.text: the action is based on an alleged violation of a right secured by the Constitution of the United States;
jurisdiction is based in whole or in part on ; orsection 1343 of this title
the relief sought consists of money damages in an amount greater than $150,000.
consent to arbitration is freely and knowingly obtained; and
no party or attorney is prejudiced for refusing to participate in arbitration.

children.heading: .—Presumptions
children.label: c .—Presumptions
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(3), a district court may presume damages are not in excess of $150,000 unless counsel certifies that damages exceed such amount.

children.heading: .—Existing Programs
children.label: d .—Existing Programs
children.text: Nothing in this chapter is deemed to affect any program in which arbitration is conducted pursuant to section title IX of the Judicial Improvements and Access to Justice Act (), as amended by .1Public Law 100–702section 1 of Public Law 105–53So in original. The word “section” probably should not appear.1
